title: Computational Materials Design for Room-Temperature Superconductivity
executive_result: Phonon-only Eliashberg calculations cannot close the 149 K gap. The benchmark (Hg1223, 151 K) remains the top candidate. Best new prediction: Sm3Ni2O7/SLAO at 26 K phonon-only.
key_findings: Phonon-only oxide Tc ceiling: 30-40 K (fundamental, not computational)
Hg1223 (n=3) is optimal in Hg family; AF kills n>=4 inner planes
Nickelate strain enhances dz2 sigma-bonding: Tc(phonon) goes from 17 to 26 K
Hybrid superlattice: volume-dilution of lambda prevents Tc exceeding parents
Spin fluctuations provide 55-80% of total pairing in both families
next_steps_priority_order: 1. Reproduce Hg1223 PQP at 151 K (highest ROI experiment)
3. Grow Sm3Ni2O7 on SLAO (test new prediction)
4. RPA spin-fluctuation for La3Ni2O7 (cheaper than DMFT)
honest_assessment: No phonon-only computation can predict room-temperature superconductivity in oxides. The 30-40 K phonon ceiling is a fundamental limitation of oxide phonon frequencies. Room-temperature superconductivity in this materials class, if achievable at all, requires the spin-fluctuation channel. DMFT+Eliashberg is the minimum computational method needed for quantitative Tc prediction. The most productive experimental step is reproducing the existing 151 K benchmark.
